title: The Rebellion Regroups After Udon
description: On pages 3–6, this part focuses on Unknown person #1, Law, Unknown person #2, Unknown person #3, Unknown person #4 as After Udon’s fall, the alliance regroups, hidden allies surface, and the Kozuki rebellion becomes more concrete.
scene: After Udon’s fall, the alliance regroups, hidden allies surface, and the Kozuki rebellion becomes more concrete.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Wano Country Flower Capital

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: So you're telling me you let them go?!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: From right under your noses?!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Forgive me, my LORD!

dialogue.speaker: Law
dialogue.text: But, the man known as TraFAlgar Law...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: is as much of a criminal mastermind as straw hat himself! Capturing him is as good as netting the both of them!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Betrayal is second-nature to pirates!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: After torture, I am sure he will be of much use to us.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Very well, then!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: How are things in the capital?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: Do not worry!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: The entire area is on Lockdown.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: The Samurai of the Minawarigumi and my Ninja have been stationed around the capital.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Until we have captured every last one of these investors,

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I will not let you come to any harm, my LORD!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: You're Tellin' Me All Those People Are Wanted?!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Citizens that Harbor or fail to report sightings

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: of these criminals will be considered accessory to their crimes!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: This isn't good. I doubt we'll be able to approach the capital at all anymore.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: The wanted orders are effective across the country.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Not just here. In any case, I suppose you could say we no longer have any business left in the capital.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: We were tasked with finding Samurai with the mark of the moon.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: Now all of 'em have been captured.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: And here we thought that tonyasu's acting was enough to take the blame off them.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: We may have been a bit optimistic.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: The Jailhouse is quite large. By my reckoning,

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: They've got 1000 men locked up.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Say, these guys are only locked up because the Shogun's men messed up, right?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: Why won't he just let 'em free?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: And admit he was wrong? Lord Crochi? Come on, man. Never.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: We know they're on our side, too.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: What a shame. We've gotta do something.

dialogue.speaker: Law
dialogue.text: law's men should be in there, too.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: By the way, Shinobu...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: What are they doing over there?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: making sky-boats. It's for the fire festival.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Surprised people are still in the festival spirit considering that two famous public figures have just died.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: The opposite, in fact.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: The fire festival is a ceremony to mourn the dead.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: The grander it is, the better our feelings are said to reach those who have passed...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: I see...

summary: This section advances the Wano - Rebellion Regroups storyline with 41 dialogue beats. After Udon’s fall, the alliance regroups, hidden allies surface, and the Kozuki rebellion becomes more concrete.
